---
title: "Demo Walkthroughs"
---

Step-by-step guides for the three built-in demos.
Every command works out of the box with `--no-llm`; add an `OPENAI_API_KEY` to unlock LLM-generated reports.

## Activity Recognition (synthetic data) {#synth}

### 1. Generate data and run the demo

```bash
uv run ts-agents demo window-classification --no-llm
```

This single command:

1. Generates a synthetic labeled-stream CSV (`data/demo_labeled_stream.csv`)
2. Sweeps candidate window sizes and scores each with a windowed classifier
3. Evaluates the best window on a held-out test split
4. Writes plots and a Markdown report

### 2. Interpret the outputs

All artifacts land in `outputs/demo/` by default.

| Artifact | What it tells you |
|----------|-------------------|
| `window_selection.json` | Per-window scores and the chosen `best_window_size` |
| `window_scores.png` | Visual: score vs window size |
| `eval.json` | Final metrics — accuracy, F1, confusion matrix, class counts |
| `confusion_matrix.png` | Heatmap of predicted vs true labels |
| `report.md` | One-page Markdown summary (classifier, best window, scores) |

### 3. Customize the run

```bash
# Different synthetic scenario (gait, stairs, industrial)
uv run ts-agents demo window-classification --no-llm --scenario gait

# Narrower window sweep with a specific classifier and metric
uv run ts-agents demo window-classification --no-llm \
  --window-sizes 32,64,128 \
  --classifier minirocket \
  --metric balanced_accuracy
```

Key flags:

| Flag | Default | Options |
|------|---------|---------|
| `--scenario` | `stairs` | `gait`, `stairs`, `industrial` |
| `--window-sizes` | `32,64,96,128,160` | Comma-separated integers |
| `--classifier` | `auto` | `auto`, `minirocket`, `rocket`, `knn` |
| `--metric` | `balanced_accuracy` | `accuracy`, `balanced_accuracy`, `f1_macro` |
| `--output-dir` | `outputs/demo` | Any path |

### 4. LLM-backed variant

Drop the `--no-llm` flag. The agent calls the same tools but writes a richer narrative report.

```bash
export OPENAI_API_KEY="sk-..."
uv run ts-agents demo window-classification
```

---

## Activity Recognition (WISDM data) {#wisdm}

This WISDM walkthrough is a **source-checkout workflow**. It uses the repo-root
dataset `data/wisdm_subset.csv`, which is not bundled into the published wheel.

### Quick start

```bash
bash demo/run_demo_wisdm.sh
```

The script runs window-size selection, picks the best window, evaluates, and generates plots — all under `outputs/demo_wisdm/`.

### Build a custom WISDM stream

To download the full WISDM dataset and build your own labeled stream:

```bash
python data/make_demo_labeled_stream_wisdm.py \
  --subject 1600 --device watch --sensor accel \
  --activities walking,jogging,sitting,standing \
  --trim-policy per_class_seconds \
  --per-class-seconds walking=180,jogging=60,sitting=180,standing=180 \
  --out data/demo_labeled_stream.csv
```

Then run the demo on that CSV:

```bash
uv run ts-agents demo window-classification --no-llm \
  --csv-path data/demo_labeled_stream.csv
```

Output artifacts are the same as the synthetic demo: `window_selection.json`, `window_scores.png`, `eval.json`, `confusion_matrix.png`, and `report.md`.

---

## Forecasting Comparison {#forecasting}

### 1. Run the demo

```bash
uv run ts-agents demo forecasting --no-llm
```

Compares forecasting methods on a built-in test series, writes metrics and a plot.

### 2. Interpret the outputs

Artifacts land in `outputs/demo/` by default.

| Artifact | What it tells you |
|----------|-------------------|
| `forecast_comparison.json` | Per-method RMSE, MAE, MAPE and the best method |
| `forecast_comparison.png` | Overlay plot of each method's forecast vs actuals |
| `forecasting_report.md` | Markdown table with metrics and a recommendation |

Example report snippet:

```
Run ID: Re200Rm200 | Variable: bx001_real | Horizon: 1
Compared Methods: arima, theta | Best Method (RMSE): theta
arima: RMSE=0.0294, MAE=0.0294, MAPE=18.36%
theta: RMSE=0.0108, MAE=0.0108, MAPE=6.74%
Recommendation: THETA (RMSE: 0.0108)
```

### 3. Customize the run

```bash
# More methods, longer horizon, full dataset
uv run ts-agents demo forecasting --no-llm \
  --methods arima,ets,theta \
  --horizon 12 \
  --full-data
```

Key flags:

| Flag | Default | Options |
|------|---------|---------|
| `--methods` | `arima,theta` | `arima`, `ets`, `theta` (comma-separated) |
| `--horizon` | `1` | Any positive integer |
| `--full-data` | off | Use the full dataset instead of the test subset |
| `--output-dir` | `outputs/demo` | Any path |

### 4. LLM-backed report generation

```bash
export OPENAI_API_KEY="sk-..."
uv run ts-agents demo forecasting
```

The LLM reads the comparison JSON and writes a narrative report with context and recommendations.
